page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2192.0, placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show strong performance, registering 34.63 GB/sec and 14.54 GB/sec respectively. However, multi-core performance is comparatively limited; the stress-ng multi-core test and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory performance is mixed, with cached small-block read bandwidth averaging 353.92 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read bandwidth drops to a weaker 36.01 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio coupled with high-frequency single-core execution, making it a specialized option for workloads that do not scale linearly across many cores but require rapid processing of memory-resident data. Tradeoffs include weak multi-core scaling and the absence of local NVMe storage. This instance is suited for single-threaded or low-concurrency applications, memory-bound tasks, small-scale database operations, and light LLM inference using small models.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6455B processor clocked at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2192.0, and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is also relatively weak at 36.01 GB/sec. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high clock speed, the r7iz.xlarge is suited for memory-intensive workloads with low parallelization requirements, such as single-threaded data processing, small database operations, and light LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, offering top-tier single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and high-frequency Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining high-frequency single-core Intel Xeon performance with DDR5 memory for specialized, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6455B processor clocked at 3.9 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs
32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2192.0
Tradeoffs include weak multi-core scaling and no local storage
Suited for memory-bound, single-threaded applications and light LLM inference
